what_it_does: Implements the `company` command-line dispatcher: parses `sys.argv`, resolves service targets, enforces live GPU-VRAM and system-RAM capacity invariants, orchestrates systemd start/stop/restart, records per-service load telemetry, and exposes thin operator interfaces for suite health, coherence scans, model management, sessions, internal board items, and clone records.
observations: `main()` is a large, sequential dispatcher with many `if cmd == ...` branches and inline imports for optional subcommands.

Lifecycle actions (`up`, `down`, `restart`) are handled by `_act()`, which calls `gpu.check_fit()`, optionally `gpu.plan_eviction()`, and separately `gpu.ram_fit()`; `--force` overrides only the VRAM refusal, not RAM.
`_wait_and_record()` polls ports until services serve and records telemetry only for a single GPU-trackable service; concurrent starts are confirmed serving but skip VRAM attribution.
A commented 'MEASURED-RESTART FIX' tears a service down before the pre-start VRAM snapshot on `restart --wait` so the old resident instance is not counted.
`company suites` shells the all-green gate suite under a temporary store and propagates its exit code.
`company coherence` imports `runtime.coherence_detect` and `runtime.orienteering_drift` and prints their scans.
`company ensure` delegates to `capabilities.ensure_resident` and exits with code 3 when a swap/eviction requires explicit `--evict` approval.
Combo targets (`@name`) are validated against hardware capacity before the live resource gate.
Session, board, and clone subcommands are thin wrappers that import `sessions`, `board`, and `clone` modules respectively.
standouts: Single-file monolithic CLI dispatcher with manual `sys.argv` flag splitting.
Dual-capacity invariant enforcement: GPU VRAM plus live `/proc/meminfo` system RAM, with different override rules.
Telemetry recording deliberately avoids garbage attribution by skipping concurrent GPU loads.
All-green gate and coherence read are surfaced as operator-facing wrappers from the same console.
conventions: Follows a docstring-heavy module header that lists every supported command.
Follows a thin-wrapper pattern for suites, coherence, session, board, and clone by delegating to dedicated modules.
Breaks the small-function convention: `main()` mixes parsing, validation, dispatch, and I/O in one long routine.
Uses `sys.exit()` for errors and early returns rather than exceptions for command failures.
Uses project-specific modules (`registry`, `systemd`, `gpu`, `models`, etc.) instead of third-party CLI frameworks.
concerns: `main()` is long and mixes many responsibilities, making the command matrix harder to test and extend.
Inline imports inside command branches obscure the file's dependency surface.
Hard-coded relative paths (`.venv/bin/python`, `tests/suite_health_acceptance.py`, `runtime/...`) derived from `__file__` will break if the repository layout changes.
Manual flag parsing with `startswith('--')` only supports boolean flags and could mis-handle flags with attached values if they are added later.
`_wait_and_record()` calls `systemd.port_open()` and `systemd.is_active()` repeatedly every 3 seconds; the exact contract of those return values is not visible here.
notes: The `company` console dispatcher: parses commands, enforces GPU/RAM capacity, starts and stops services, records telemetry, and thinly exposes suite, coherence, session, board, clone, and model-management workflows.
questions: What is the exact schema returned by `registry.load()` for services, groups, and combos?
What are the precise return shapes of `gpu.check_fit()`, `gpu.plan_eviction()`, and `gpu.ram_fit()`?
How does `capabilities.ensure_resident()` mutate state and what does `EnsureResidentError` cover?
How are model-swap changes persisted and validated in `models.swap()`?
What headroom does `gpu.ram_fit()` subtract from `MemAvailable`, and how are combo capacity estimates computed?
apparent_themes: Single operator console for the whole local system
Fail-loud resource management with capacity invariants
On-demand service lifecycle with no auto-start
Explicit approval gates for destructive or state-changing actions
Thin wrappers to keep specialized logic in its owning module
intention_signals: Provide a unified, safe, observable control plane for local AI services where every start is capacity-checked, every solo load can be measured, and every broad workflow has a single CLI entry point.
novelty: A local systemd-based service orchestrator combined with live GPU/RAM capacity invariants, a measured-restart telemetry fix, and integrated all-green gate, coherence scan, session fleet, noticeboard, and clone read surfaces in one CLI.
contribution: This file contributes the CLI command routing and user-facing orchestration ritual: argument parsing, target resolution, capacity-gated start/stop/restart, telemetry recording for solo loads, and thin operator interfaces to testing, coherence, session fleet, internal noticeboard, and clone records.
summary_for_embedding: ops/cli/app.py is the `company` command-line dispatcher that parses subcommands, resolves service targets, enforces live GPU-VRAM and system-RAM capacity invariants, orchestrates systemd service lifecycle, records per-service load telemetry, and exposes thin wrappers for suite health, coherence scans, model management, sessions, board items, and clone records.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to give a single, safe, observable console for starting, stopping, budgeting, and inspecting local Company services and their supporting workflows.

symbols.does: Filters a list of service keys to those that occupy GPU VRAM and expose a telemetry-trackable port.
symbols.description: Returns the subset of keys whose registry entries have a non-zero VRAM estimate (via `vram_of`) and a configured port. This is used by `_wait_and_record()` to decide which started services should be health-checked and possibly measured.

symbols.does: Blocks until each GPU service's port is open, then records load-time and measured VRAM telemetry for solo loads only.
symbols.description: Polls `systemd.port_open()` and `systemd.is_active()` for GPU-trackable services. If exactly one GPU service was started, it computes the VRAM delta from the pre-start free snapshot and records elapsed time, measured resident VRAM, and estimated VRAM via `telemetry.record()`. Concurrent starts are confirmed serving but skip VRAM attribution because the delta cannot be attributed per service.

symbols.does: Executes up/down/restart actions on a resolved set of services after GPU and RAM capacity checks.
symbols.description: For `up`/`restart` it prints GPU state, checks fit via `gpu.check_fit()`, optionally plans and executes largest-first eviction via `gpu.plan_eviction()`, allows `--force` to override a VRAM refusal, and separately enforces system RAM via `gpu.ram_fit()` (which `--force` cannot bypass). It then tears down or controls each service through `systemd`/`gpu`, and if `--wait` is requested it snapshots free VRAM and delegates to `_wait_and_record()`.

symbols.does: Parses `sys.argv` and dispatches to the appropriate `company` CLI subcommand handler.
symbols.description: Loads the registry, identifies the command, and either handles it inline (status, gpu, health, combos, config, ensure, up/down/restart, logs, help) or thinly delegates to project modules for suites, coherence, models, telemetry, session, board, clone, swap, and bench. It resolves targets, validates combo hardware fit, and invokes `_act()` for lifecycle commands.
